real_insight: There is an operationally describable career-state — the senior whose composure does not collapse under either failure (worst-incident post-mortem) or success (clean launch + recruiter spike) — and the chapter has been arguing toward this state since 2.11. The verse names its terminal completion. The engineering analog scopes deliberately downward (the metaphysical brāhmī-sthiti / brahma-nirvāṇa is past where engineering travels) and is explicit about the scope-honesty.
objection: The 'twenty-five years in' framing is on the line of wisdom-theater — anyone can say 'wait 25 years and you'll be unflappable'. The analog earns it by giving a specific named scenario (Kubernetes control-plane outage cascading through Postgres connection-pool exhaustion into a four-hour billing-service failure; Datadog red, Sentry climbing, executive Slack asking for status every fifteen minutes). The engineer's behavior is operationally specified: she walks the timeline, names the cascade, does not blame the on-call who paged her at hour two and made the wrong call at hour three, does not defend her own architectural decision from five years prior. These are concrete, named professional behaviors. The launch-side scenario is similarly specific. The STRETCHED tag is honest — the metaphysical brāhmī-sthiti is past engineering. The falsifiability clause explicitly disclaims that engineering tenure produces brāhmī-sthiti. A hostile reviewer cannot demand more honesty than this verse-record provides. Pass.
